pattern: Manacher gives the longest odd palindrome at every center; from it derive, for each split point, the best odd palindrome wholly on the left and on the right, then maximize the product across all splits.
hints: Both substrings must have odd length, so you only ever care about odd-length palindromes centered at each index.
Compute the longest odd palindrome at every center in linear time, then for each cut between two indices combine the best palindrome entirely to its left with the best entirely to its right.

A palindrome ending at position j shrinks to one ending at j-1 with length minus two; propagate these to get best-ending-at and best-starting-at arrays, take prefix and suffix maxima, and scan every split point for the maximum product.

edge_inputs.description: two length-three palindromes give product nine
two distinct characters, each a length-one palindrome
two identical characters cannot overlap so product is one
one long palindrome must be split into smaller pieces
no multi-char palindrome means product of two singletons

stress.description: small-alphabet strings of moderate length so many overlapping odd palindromes appear
stress.note: size capped so the quadratic oracle stays fast; tiny alphabet maximises palindrome density

stress.description: three-letter alphabet for varied palindrome structure
